name: Cream Pair — pomegranate + saffron
mood: Both on cream. Tandoori = pomegranate-red accents; Masala = saffron-gold. Shared editorial base, heat-coded.

name: Dark Pair — oxblood + espresso
mood: Premium dark shelf. Tandoori = deep oxblood, Masala = charcoal-espresso. Both read expensive and night-market.

name: Earth Pair — terracotta + bone
mood: Warm neutrals, farm-shop heritage. Tandoori = clay terracotta, Masala = bone-cream. Both artisanal, heat-coded by warmth.

name: Herbal Pair — chilli-green + sage-cream
mood: Spice-grocer lineage. Tandoori = deep chilli-green stock, Masala = sage-on-cream. Herbal, the explicit anti-MRE.

name: Saffron Pair — deep saffron + cardamom gold
mood: Warm golds, different saturations. Tandoori = deep saffron, Masala = softer cardamom-gold. South-Asian kitchen confidence, calibrated.

name: Sunset Pair — blush-peach + dusk-lilac
mood: Soft pastels, unexpected. Tandoori = blush-peach stock, Masala = dusk-lilac. Reads young, DTC, photo-friendly.

name: Paper Pair — warm ivory + cool ecru
mood: Near-white on near-white. Tandoori = warm ivory, Masala = cool ecru. Editorial restraint, accent does all the work.

name: Spice-Market Pair — brick-red + mustard
mood: Shouty market-stall colour. Tandoori = deep brick-red stock, Masala = mustard stock. Reads from across the shop.

name: Monochrome Pair — warm grey + cool grey
mood: Both neutral. Tandoori = warm-toned grey stock, Masala = cool-toned grey. Minimalist, accent colour is the only signal.

name: Night-Market Pair — burgundy + ink-teal
mood: Both deep, pub-shelf confident. Tandoori = deep burgundy, Masala = ink-teal. Premium, masculine, after-dark.
